message:
Split rename type-mismatch E2E contract into per-direction helpers

`assert_rename_file_directory_type_mismatch_contract` covered two
independent mismatch scenarios in one body:
  1. renaming a regular file over an existing directory must fail
     with `EISDIR` and leave both entries untouched.
  2. renaming a directory over an existing regular file must fail
     with `ENOTDIR` and leave both entries untouched.

Both legs snapshot root entries, source, target-child, and
target-directory state before the rename, verify the exact errno, and
assert nothing on disk or in the directory listing moved. The
combined function was long enough to need `#[allow(clippy::
too_many_lines)]`, and the two halves reused the same `err` /
`root_entries_before` pattern under different local names
(`file_over_dir_err`, `dir_over_file_err`,
`file_over_dir_entries_before`, `dir_over_file_entries_before`).

This change extracts:
- `assert_rename_file_over_directory_rejected_with_eisdir` — the
  file-over-dir leg.
- `assert_rename_directory_over_file_rejected_with_enotdir` — the
  dir-over-file leg.

The public scenario entry point now just sequences the two helpers
and emits the same `PASS` record
(`file_over_dir=EISDIR_dir_over_file=ENOTDIR_no_drift`). Local
variable names collapse to plain `err` / `root_entries_before` inside
each helper, which is now unambiguous. No assertions or tolerated
behaviors change — this is purely a readability/lint-debt cleanup and
drops the `too_many_lines` allow.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
